title: Spinning up containers from flask compose sample with docker-compose does not work with podman endpoint
body:
### Issue Description

I am trying to run `docker-compose up` with a DOCKER_HOST set to a podman endpoint on windows but it ends up stuck on `sending tarball` line in the log. Please see the details in podman desktop repo.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Please see https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/issues/3563.



### Describe the results you received

...
#19 [frontend stage-3 4/5] RUN rm -rf ./*
#19 DONE 0.3s

#20 [frontend stage-3 5/5] COPY --from=build /app/build .
#20 DONE 0.2s

#21 [frontend] exporting to docker image format
#21 exporting layers
#21 exporting layers 0.8s done
#21 exporting manifest sha256:74344854197fa66dfc520d79e8ef177c1ae6f33300afd6d15126af045560b7d1 0.1s done
#21 exporting config sha256:8e50c5588fcc60b7726f1615d9239d2e8fd9fd28f7cc1d0f2834ba58e7e501e6 0.0s done
#21 sending tarball

### Describe the results you expected

`compose up` to be finished successfully.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 10, podman 4.6.1 (client), server: 4.6.0.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @rhatdan That is a good question. I will try to find out. In a mean time, I manage to make it run on different machine. A bit more generous with a resources (32GB against 16GB on previous), though.
@odockal does it work on another machine?
@vrothberg Yes it did work on different machine.
I ran into this issue while trying to spin up a .Net & MSSQL devcontainer images w/ VS Code.  It hangs on sending tarball and workspace folder size is roughly 25MB.
I would assume this is the same problem described in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20379
